name: Darrell Castle & Associates
description_short: Memphis-based bankruptcy and personal injury law firm offering Chapter 7/13 bankruptcy, foreclosure defense, and accident representation with 39 years of experience.
description_long:
Darrell Castle & Associates is a long-established law firm based in Memphis, Tennessee, founded in 1984. The firm specializes in consumer bankruptcy representation and personal injury litigation, serving clients across Shelby, Fayette, Lauderdale, and Tipton counties. Over nearly four decades, they have built a reputation for handling high-volume bankruptcy filings while maintaining a client-focused approach.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 (liquidation) and Chapter 13 (reorganization) filings, as well as related debt-relief services such as stopping wage garnishments, foreclosure defense, and car repossession prevention. On the personal injury side, they handle auto accidents, truck accidents, product liability, dangerous drugs, medical malpractice, and workers' compensation claims. All initial consultations are free, and they offer 24/7 call answering with direct attorney access.

The firm emphasizes client communication and satisfaction as their differentiator. They explicitly state they are "big enough to investigate your claim" but "small enough to treat you with respect and communicate every step of the way." They hold a 5-star Avvo rating, A+ Better Business Bureau rating, and Martindale-Hubbell Client Distinction Awards. They operate on contingency for personal injury cases (no fee unless you win) and charge no extra fees for phone calls or additional support during bankruptcy representation.

As a law firm rather than a debt relief or financial services company, Darrell Castle & Associates serves consumers who need legal representation rather than alternative financial products. Their longevity and awards suggest legitimacy, though prospective clients should note that bankruptcy filing outcomes depend heavily on individual financial circumstances, and personal injury cases require meeting statute of limitations deadlines.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 39 years of bankruptcy experience (since 1984) with thousands of completed cases
5-star Avvo rating and A+ Better Business Bureau rating with Martindale-Hubbell Client Distinction Awards
Free initial consultations with no obligation to hire
24/7 call answering with direct attorney access (not just intake staff)
No additional fees for phone calls or extra support during bankruptcy cases
Contingency-based personal injury representation (no fee unless you win)
Multi-county service area (Shelby, Fayette, Lauderdale, Tipton counties)
cons: Limited to Tennessee service area despite operating for 39 years; no national or multi-state presence
No pricing information provided on website; bankruptcy filing costs and attorney fees not transparently listed
Personal injury cases subject to statute of limitations—delay in contacting them could eliminate legal rights
Mixed practice (bankruptcy + personal injury) may mean less specialization than single-focus firms
Reviews and testimonials not visible on public website; ratings require visiting third-party sites (Avvo, BBB)
best_for: Memphis and surrounding county residents facing medical debt, credit card debt, or foreclosure who want experienced local bankruptcy counsel
Tennessee accident victims needing personal injury representation with a firm that emphasizes communication and case investigation
Consumers experiencing wage garnishment or car repossession seeking immediate legal intervention
Individuals hesitant about bankruptcy who want free, detailed guidance before making a decision
diagnosis: Darrell Castle & Associates is appropriate for Memphis-area residents needing bankruptcy legal representation or personal injury counsel from an established, credentialed firm. The main caveat is that they are a law firm providing legal services, not a financial services company or debt relief alternative—bankruptcy outcomes depend on individual finances and eligibility, and personal injury cases require timely action before statute of limitations expire.
services: Dangerous drugs litigation (including talcum powder cases)
Social Security Disability (SSDI) application and appeal assistance
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
